habitat: Subtropical coastlines; offshore islands; tidal mudflats; and pelagic waters
story: Explore the fascinating world of the Chinese Crested Tern (Thalasseus bernsteini). Classified under the Laridae family, this bird predominantly inhabits Subtropical coastlines; offshore islands; tidal mudflats; and pelagic waters. The current IUCN classification of Critically Endangered (CR) underscores the importance of safeguarding its natural range. From its feeding habits to its nesting preferences, the Chinese Crested Tern is a testament to the intricate web of life. By studying species like the Chinese Crested Tern, we gain valuable insights into broader ecological trends.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
